title: dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella, commonly known as the Purple Marsh Orchid, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The Purple Marsh Orchid is said to possess therapeutic actions such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
benefits: Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella has many benefits, such as its potential to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. This plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can protect cells from damage and reduce the risk of certain cancers, such as colon and breast cancer. Additionally, Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella may help to improve cognitive function and memory, and its antiseptic properties may aid in wound healing and prevent infections. By incorporating this plant into one's lifestyle, individuals may experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to a better quality of life and increased energy levels.

constituents: Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin), phenolic acids (gallic acid, protocatechuic acid, and caffeic acid), alkaloids (orchinol, dactylorhizin, and rhizome-lysine), and saponins (dihydro-β-sitosterol and β-sitosterol). parts: Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella has many medicinal parts, such as the tubers, which contain glycosides like purpurin and dactylin. The leaves of this plant are also used in traditional medicine, rich in flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, as well as phenolic acids. The flowers of Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella contain flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, along with phenolic acids and terpenoids. The roots of this plant are a rich source of saponins and phenolic acids, which are used to treat various health conditions.
preparations: Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that have been traditionally used for various medicinal purposes. Teas made from the plant are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Tinctures of Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella are used to treat cardiovascular conditions, such as high blood pressure and heart palpitations, as it is believed to have a positive effect on the cardiovascular system. side_effects: Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides. Excessive consumption may also lead to cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat and decreased blood pressure. In some cases, using Dactylorhiza majalis var. purpurella improperly may cause allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and itching.
